task:
Create a task plan for restructuring the pi-skills monorepo. 

## Context

This is a pi package (agent skills) at /Users/josorio/Code/pi-skills. It contains TypeScript skills that need restructuring.

## Current State

- Root `package.json` has all shared deps (`exa-js`, `@types/node`, `tsx`, `typescript`)
- Root `tsconfig.json` covers `skills/**/*.ts` but uses `module: ES2022` / `moduleResolution: bundler`
- `skills/exa-search/scripts/` has 6 TS files: `lib/common.ts` (shared utils — mix of pure core + CLI glue) and 5 CLI scripts (`answer.ts`, `search.ts`, `contents.ts`, `find-similar.ts`, `research.ts`)
- `skills/posthog-skill/` has its own `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml` with identical devDeps to root. Source: `scripts/run.ts` (CLI), `scripts/lib/posthog-client.ts` (pure API client, 18 types, zero imports), `scripts/lib/dashboard-spec.ts` (pure data, zero imports), `scripts/lib/fixtures.ts` (depends on dashboard-spec only). Tests: `scripts/__tests__/` (8 test files — 2 unit, 6 integration via spawnSync)

## Target State

### Single root config
- ONE `package.json` at root — all deps, test scripts, no per-skill package.json
- ONE `tsconfig.json` at root — unified config using `module: Node16`, `moduleResolution: Node16`, `noEmit: true`, strict options (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`), `types: ["node"]`, `include: ["skills/**/*.ts"]`
- Delete posthog-skill's `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `node_modules`
- Root scripts: `"typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"`, `"test:posthog": "tsx --test skills/posthog-skill/__tests__/**/*.test.ts skills/posthog-skill/scripts/__tests__/**/*.test.ts"`

### exa-search restructure
Split `skills/exa-search/scripts/lib/common.ts` into:
- `skills/exa-search/src/index.ts` — re-exports from core modules
- `skills/exa-search/src/client.ts` — `createClient()` (pure, wraps `new Exa()`)
- `skills/exa-search/src/options.ts` — `filterOptions()`, `buildContentsOptions()` (pure transforms)
- `skills/exa-search/scripts/cli.ts` — CLI-only helpers: `parseArgs`, `requireApiKey`, `requireArg`, `showHelp`, `handleError`, `executeAndPrint`
- Update all 5 CLI scripts to import core from `../src/index.js` and CLI helpers from `./cli.js`
- Delete `scripts/lib/common.ts` and `scripts/lib/` directory

### posthog-skill restructure
Move core library from `scripts/lib/` to `src/`:
- `skills/posthog-skill/src/index.ts` — re-exports
- `skills/posthog-skill/src/client.ts` — from `scripts/lib/posthog-client.ts` (282 lines, 18 types + createClient)
- `skills/posthog-skill/src/dashboard-spec.ts` — from `scripts/lib/dashboard-spec.ts` (191 lines, pure data)
- `skills/posthog-skill/src/fixtures.ts` — from `scripts/lib/fixtures.ts` (131 lines, depends on dashboard-spec)
- Move unit tests: `scripts/__tests__/posthog-client.test.ts` → `__tests__/client.test.ts`, `scripts/__tests__/dashboard-spec.test.ts` → `__tests__/dashboard-spec.test.ts`
- Update `scripts/run.ts` imports from `./lib/posthog-client.js` → `../src/index.js` (or specific modules)
- Update unit test imports to point to `../src/`
- Integration tests stay in `scripts/__tests__/` — update hardcoded `../../node_modules/.bin/tsx` path to `../../../../node_modules/.bin/tsx` (resolves to root node_modules)
- Delete `scripts/lib/` directory

### Fix imports for Node16 module resolution
The root tsconfig changes to `module: Node16` / `moduleResolution: Node16`. Check all existing imports in exa-search scripts — they already use `.js` extensions. Verify posthog-skill imports also use `.js` extensions. Any bare imports without `.js` need to be fixed.

### Update .gitignore
Add `.turbo` if needed (actually no turbo in this plan). Ensure `node_modules` and `dist` are covered.

### Update SKILL.md files
- posthog-skill SKILL.md: change setup from "run `pnpm install` in the skill directory" to "run `npm install` at the repo root"
- exa-search SKILL.md: update setup instructions similarly, update script paths if needed

## Constraints
- This is a pi package — `pi.skills: ["./skills"]` in root package.json must stay
- Skills are discovered by SKILL.md files — directory structure under skills/ matters
- All scripts run via `tsx` (shebang `#!/usr/bin/env tsx`) — no compilation step
- No workspaces, no turbo, no monorepo tooling
- Tests use Node.js built-in test runner (`node:test` + `node:assert/strict`) run via `tsx --test`
- Integration tests spawn `tsx scripts/run.ts` via `spawnSync` — the tsx binary path matters
- posthog-skill unit tests use mock fetch injection via `createClient(config, mockFetch)`
- exa-search has NO tests currently

## Rules
- TDD: write failing tests first for any new code, but this is a restructure — existing tests must pass after the move
- Use `npm pkg set` to modify package.json, never edit directly
- Use `edit` for existing files, `write` for new files
- Run typecheck and tests after changes to verify nothing broke

Create a task plan for restructuring the pi-skills monorepo. 

## Context

This is a pi package (agent skills) at /Users/josorio/Code/pi-skills. It contains TypeScript skills that need restructuring.

## Current State

- Root `package.json` has all shared deps (`exa-js`, `@types/node`, `tsx`, `typescript`)
- Root `tsconfig.json` covers `skills/**/*.ts` but uses `module: ES2022` / `moduleResolution: bundler`
- `skills/exa-search/scripts/` has 6 TS files: `lib/common.ts` (shared utils — mix of pure core + CLI glue) and 5 CLI scripts (`answer.ts`, `search.ts`, `contents.ts`, `find-similar.ts`, `research.ts`)
- `skills/posthog-skill/` has its own `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml` with identical devDeps to root. Source: `scripts/run.ts` (CLI), `scripts/lib/posthog-client.ts` (pure API client, 18 types, zero imports), `scripts/lib/dashboard-spec.ts` (pure data, zero imports), `scripts/lib/fixtures.ts` (depends on dashboard-spec only). Tests: `scripts/__tests__/` (8 test files — 2 unit, 6 integration via spawnSync)

## Target State

### Single root config
- ONE `package.json` at root — all deps, test scripts, no per-skill package.json
- ONE `tsconfig.json` at root — unified config using `module: Node16`, `moduleResolution: Node16`, `noEmit: true`, strict options (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`), `types: ["node"]`, `include: ["skills/**/*.ts"]`
- Delete posthog-skill's `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `node_modules`
- Root scripts: `"typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"`, `"test:posthog": "tsx --test skills/posthog-skill/__tests__/**/*.test.ts skills/posthog-skill/scripts/__tests__/**/*.test.ts"`

### exa-search restructure
Split `skills/exa-search/scripts/lib/common.ts` into:
- `skills/exa-search/src/index.ts` — re-exports from core modules
- `skills/exa-search/src/client.ts` — `createClient()` (pure, wraps `new Exa()`)
- `skills/exa-search/src/options.ts` — `filterOptions()`, `buildContentsOptions()` (pure transforms)
- `skills/exa-search/scripts/cli.ts` — CLI-only helpers: `parseArgs`, `requireApiKey`, `requireArg`, `showHelp`, `handleError`, `executeAndPrint`
- Update all 5 CLI scripts to import core from `../src/index.js` and CLI helpers from `./cli.js`
- Delete `scripts/lib/common.ts` and `scripts/lib/` directory

### posthog-skill restructure
Move core library from `scripts/lib/` to `src/`:
- `skills/posthog-skill/src/index.ts` — re-exports
- `skills/posthog-skill/src/client.ts` — from `scripts/lib/posthog-client.ts` (282 lines, 18 types + createClient)
- `skills/posthog-skill/src/dashboard-spec.ts` — from `scripts/lib/dashboard-spec.ts` (191 lines, pure data)
- `skills/posthog-skill/src/fixtures.ts` — from `scripts/lib/fixtures.ts` (131 lines, depends on dashboard-spec)
- Move unit tests: `scripts/__tests__/posthog-client.test.ts` → `__tests__/client.test.ts`, `scripts/__tests__/dashboard-spec.test.ts` → `__tests__/dashboard-spec.test.ts`
- Update `scripts/run.ts` imports from `./lib/posthog-client.js` → `../src/index.js` (or specific modules)
- Update unit test imports to point to `../src/`
- Integration tests stay in `scripts/__tests__/` — update hardcoded `../../node_modules/.bin/tsx` path to `../../../../node_modules/.bin/tsx` (resolves to root node_modules)
- Delete `scripts/lib/` directory

### Fix imports for Node16 module resolution
The root tsconfig changes to `module: Node16` / `moduleResolution: Node16`. Check all existing imports in exa-search scripts — they already use `.js` extensions. Verify posthog-skill imports also use `.js` extensions. Any bare imports without `.js` need to be fixed.

### Update .gitignore
Add `.turbo` if needed (actually no turbo in this plan). Ensure `node_modules` and `dist` are covered.

### Update SKILL.md files
- posthog-skill SKILL.md: change setup from "run `pnpm install` in the skill directory" to "run `npm install` at the repo root"
- exa-search SKILL.md: update setup instructions similarly, update script paths if needed

## Constraints
- This is a pi package — `pi.skills: ["./skills"]` in root package.json must stay
- Skills are discovered by SKILL.md files — directory structure under skills/ matters
- All scripts run via `tsx` (shebang `#!/usr/bin/env tsx`) — no compilation step
- No workspaces, no turbo, no monorepo tooling
- Tests use Node.js built-in test runner (`node:test` + `node:assert/strict`) run via `tsx --test`
- Integration tests spawn `tsx scripts/run.ts` via `spawnSync` — the tsx binary path matters
- posthog-skill unit tests use mock fetch injection via `createClient(config, mockFetch)`
- exa-search has NO tests currently

## Rules
- TDD: write failing tests first for any new code, but this is a restructure — existing tests must pass after the move
- Use `npm pkg set` to modify package.json, never edit directly
- Use `edit` for existing files, `write` for new files
- Run typecheck and tests after changes to verify nothing broke

scripts/search.ts

#!/usr/bin/env tsx
/**
 * Exa search — semantic search and search with contents.
 *
 * Usage:
 *   tsx scripts/search.ts <query> [options-json]
 *   tsx scripts/search.ts --help
 *
 * Options JSON (all optional):
 *   {
 *     "numResults": 10,
 *     "type": "auto",                  // "auto"|"fast"|"deep"|"deep-reasoning"|"deep-max"|"instant"
 *     "contents": true,                // true = text+highlights, or object for fine control
 *     "text": true,                    // shorthand: include text in results
 *     "highlights": true,              // shorthand: include highlights
 *     "summary": true,                 // shorthand: include summary
 *     "includeDomains": ["example.com"],
 *     "excludeDomains": ["spam.com"],
 *     "startPublishedDate": "2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
 *     "endPublishedDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
 *     "startCrawlDate": "2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
 *     "endCrawlDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
 *     "category": "news",             // "company"|"research paper"|"news"|"pdf"|"tweet"|"personal site"|"financial report"|"people"
 *     "includeText": ["must contain"],
 *     "excludeText": ["must not contain"],
 *     "useAutoprompt": true,
 *     "moderation": false,
 *     "userLocation": "US",
 *     "additionalQueries": ["alt query 1"],  // deep search only, max 5
 *     "outputSchema": {},              // deep search structured output
 *     "subpages": 0,
 *     "subpageTarget": "pricing",
 *     "livecrawl": "auto",              // "never"|"fallback"|"always"|"auto"|"preferred" - live-crawl pages for fresh data
 *     "livecrawlTimeout": 10000,         // timeout in ms for live crawl
 *     "maxAgeHours": 168
 *   }
 *
 * Environment:
 *   EXA_API_KEY — required
 *
 * Examples:
 *   tsx scripts/search.ts "latest AI research"
 *   tsx scripts/search.ts "AI startups" '{"numResults":5,"type":"deep","contents":true}'
 *   tsx scripts/search.ts "React best practices" '{"text":true,"highlights":true,"includeDomains":["react.dev"]}'
 */

import {
  parseArgs,
  requireApiKey,
  filterOptions,
  buildContentsOptions,
  createClient,
  executeAndPrint,
} from "./lib/common.js";

const { query, opts } = parseArgs(import.meta.url);
requireApiKey();

const exa = createClient();

// Determine if we need contents
const wantContents = opts.contents || opts.text || opts.highlights || opts.summary;

// Build contents options
const contentsOpts = buildContentsOptions(opts);

// Build search options
const searchKeys = [
  "numResults",
  "type",
  "includeDomains",
  "excludeDomains",
  "startCrawlDate",
  "endCrawlDate",
  "startPublishedDate",
  "endPublishedDate",
  "category",
  "includeText",
  "excludeText",
  "useAutoprompt",
  "moderation",
  "userLocation",
  "additionalQueries",
  "outputSchema",
  "subpages",
  "subpageTarget",
  "livecrawl",
  "livecrawlTimeout",
  "maxAgeHours",
  "filterEmptyResults",
] as const;

const searchOpts = filterOptions(opts, searchKeys);

await executeAndPrint(async () => {
  if (wantContents) {
    return await exa.searchAndContents(query, { ...searchOpts, ...contentsOpts });
  } else {
    return await exa.search(query, searchOpts);
  }
});

{
  "name": "posthog-skill",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "",
  "main": "index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "test": "tsx --test scripts/__tests__/*.test.ts",
    "test:live": "POSTHOG_TEST_LIVE=1 tsx --test scripts/__tests__/*.test.ts"
  },
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "ISC",
  "packageManager": "pnpm@10.30.1",
  "type": "module",
  "private": "true",
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^25.5.0",
    "tsx": "^4.21.0",
    "typescript": "^6.0.2"
  }
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "Node16",
    "moduleResolution": "Node16",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "sourceMap": true,
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "scripts",
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true,
    "types": ["node"]
  },
  "include": ["scripts/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}

---
name: posthog-skill
description: Automate PostHog analytics and feature flags for this project — inspect branch event availability, compare against the ACH reference insight, create or update the 7361 metrics dashboard idempotently, and manage feature flags (list, inspect, toggle, create, update, audit activity log). Use when working on analytics instrumentation, dashboards, PostHog event verification, or feature flag management.
metadata:
  author: josorio7122
  version: "1.0"
compatibility: "Requires Node.js 18+ and tsx. Run `pnpm install` in the skill directory on first use."
---

**IMPORTANT — Path Resolution:**
This is a project-local skill. `$SKILL_DIR` is the directory containing this SKILL.md file. Resolve it from where you loaded this file — do not hardcode an absolute path.

For brevity, examples below use `$RUN` as shorthand for `npx tsx $SKILL_DIR/scripts/run.ts`.

---

This skill is written in TypeScript and uses `tsx` for execution. All commands use `npx tsx` instead of `node`. Run `pnpm install` in the skill directory on first use to install `tsx` and type dependencies.

---

## Command Quick Reference

| Intent | Command |
|--------|---------|
| Check config and token status | `status` |
| See all 9 branch events (offline) | `inspect` |
| Verify events are arriving in PostHog | `inspect --live` |
| Fetch the ACH reference funnel | `compare` |
| Provision the 7361 metrics dashboard | `create` |
| List all feature flags | `flags` |

name: exa-search
description: Semantic web search, content extraction, AI-powered answers, and deep research via the Exa API. Use when you need to search documentation, look up facts, find current information, extract page content from URLs, get AI answers with citations, find pages similar to a URL, research companies or people, find LinkedIn profiles, or run deep multi-step research. Also use when the user asks to "look something up," "search the web," "find docs for," "what does X's documentation say," "research this company," or needs any information that may be more current than your training data. Requires EXA_API_KEY environment variable.
compatibility: "Requires Node.js 18+, tsx, and EXA_API_KEY environment variable set"
metadata:
  author: josorio7122
  version: "3.0"
---

# Exa Search

Semantic search, content extraction, AI answers with citations, and deep research — all via the [Exa API](https://exa.ai).

## Prerequisites

```bash
node --version   # Must be 18+
echo $EXA_API_KEY # Must be set
```

If deps are not installed, run from the skill directory:

```bash
cd <skill-dir>/skills/exa-search && npm install
```

Get an API key at: https://dashboard.exa.ai/api-keys
